text.title: Glossary Management (Living Doc) Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the glossary-management-living-doc methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Step 1 (intake): Single intake channel; capture term + scope + first usage example.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the intake gate.

rule.statement: Step 2 (define): Maintainer drafts definition with examples; status=proposed.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the define gate.

rule.statement: Step 3 (resolve conflicts): If conflicting definition exists, run conflict-resolution protocol.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the resolve conflicts gate.

rule.statement: Step 4 (publish canonical): Status=canonical; attach propagation hooks to code/tests/macros.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the publish canonical gate.

rule.statement: Step 5 (audit and deprecate): Weekly term-usage audit; deprecate ghosts with replaced_by.
rule.rationale: Skipping or reordering this step breaks the methodology's invariants; it is the audit and deprecate gate.

rule.statement: When ddd_bounded_contexts == true, do: Use scoped definitions per bounded context.
rule.rationale: Codified from the decision-tree verdict; this rule binds the gate condition to a concrete, testable action so the routing remains auditable.

rule.statement: When stakeholder_groups >= 3 AND drift_incident_count >= 1 AND maintainer_named == true, do: Stand up living-doc glossary; enforce inline-definition ban in PRDs.
rule.rationale: Codified from the decision-tree verdict; this rule binds the gate condition to a concrete, testable action so the routing remains auditable.

rule.statement: When default, do: Mark obsolete terms deprecated with replaced_by link; never delete.
rule.rationale: Codified from the decision-tree verdict; this rule binds the gate condition to a concrete, testable action so the routing remains auditable.

rule.statement: If preconditions for glossary-management-living-doc do not hold, do not apply this methodology; route to an alternative as the decision tree suggests.
rule.rationale: Methodologies misapplied to wrong contexts create overhead without value; explicit skip preserves the agent's budget.
